rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4834 as the culprit (matching yenuo26's bisection) and propose the same plausible fix (use level=1 instead of level=2 in the test), which is reasonable but unconfirmed speculation beyond what the thread actually establishes. The ground truth shows the issue still unresolved — Flink-ddd is asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode test, not confirming a fix landed. Y overreaches by flatly asserting 'this is already in main' and recommending closing as already resolved, fabricating a resolution status the thread doesn't support. X frames the same fix as a proposed diff/recommendation rather than a done deal, which is better calibrated to the actual (still-pending) thread state. Both have similar code-citation grounding and comparable completeness, with Y slightly more thorough on runtime guidance.
